failure_reason: Decision proposes two relations (project and corridor). The corridor relation (corridor_34th-st-busway has_treatment) is unsupported: neither cited block mentions the corridor entity, '34th Street Busway', or 'transitway'. p003_c0002 states off-board fare was implemented as a project milestone ('Since the last CAC, DOT & NYCT have ... Implemented off-board fare collection'), establishing only the project-treatment relation. Additionally, p004_c0002 ('Travel times reduced by 10%') does not mention off-board fare collection at all and is irrelevant to this treatment.

Decision proposes two relations (project and corridor). The corridor relation (corridor_34th-st-busway has_treatment) is unsupported: cited blocks p007_c0002, p008_c0002, and p009_c0004 describe roadway sections and design labels ('60 ft wide section', '52 ft wide section', 'Offset Bus Lane') that are elements of the SBS project design, not attributes of the corridor entity. None of the blocks mention 'corridor', 'busway', or the corridor record.

Decision proposes two relations (project and corridor). The corridor relation (corridor_34th-st-busway has_treatment) is unsupported: cited blocks p009_c0001 ('Bus Bulb') is a photo label within the SBS design presentation; p020_c0002, p021_c0002, p024_c0002 enumerate bus bulbs as construction elements in project phasing sections (West/Middle/East). These blocks document SBS project construction scope, not corridor attributes. None mention the corridor entity.

Decision proposes two relations (project and corridor). The corridor relation (corridor_34th-st-busway has_treatment) is unsupported: p009_c0005 ('Loading Zone') is a photo label; p010_c0002 documents CAC/public feedback about loading access needs in the context of the SBS project. Neither block states that the corridor entity has loading zones. The blocks establish project-context treatment, not corridor ownership.

Decision proposes two relations (project and corridor). Both are problematic. (1) Corridor relation fails for the same reason as decisions 1-4: p005_c0002 does not mention the corridor entity. (2) Additionally, MTA Bus Time is a route-level technology treatment: the block states 'Will be implemented on M34, with expansion citywide' — this is a route-level deployment (M34 = a bus route), not a physical corridor treatment. A real-time bus information system accessed via web/text/smartphone is not a feature of the physical corridor roadway.
